comment_text.sn35.4:1.5: “Sounds” are mentioned as “exterior” (bāhya) in Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 2.4.7, as opposed to the Divinity that has neither interior nor exterior (2.5.19). This anticipates the Buddhist association of bāhira with sense objects. This particular passage is full of echoes with Buddhism.

root_text.sn35.4:1.4: Yadanattā taṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn35.4:1.12: Yadanattā taṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn35.4:1.13: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpesupi nibbindati, saddesupi nibbindati, gandhesupi nibbindati, rasesupi nibbindati, phoṭṭhabbesupi nibbindati, dhammesupi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.4:1.14: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.4:1.15: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.4:1.4: And what’s not-self should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn35.4:1.12: And what’s not-self should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn35.4:1.13: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with sights, sounds, smells, tastes, touches, and ideas.
translation_text.sn35.4:1.14: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn35.4:1.15: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”

reference_text.sn35.4:1.1: dr18.3, ms13S4_9, msdiv4, pts-vp-pli4.3, sya18.3, vri26.3
